revision.comment: この項目は、神道に関連した書きかけの項目です。この記事を加筆・訂正などしてくださる協力者を求めています（ポータル 神道/ウィキプロジェクト 神道）。
revision.text: <big>'''金刀比羅神社（久慈市）'''</big> 金刀比羅神社（ことひらじんじゃ）は、岩手県久慈市湊町にある神社。 こんぴらさんの名称で親しまれている。 高台に鎮座し久慈湾を正面に望み、初日の出を拝むには最高の場所である。また津波避難場所として指定されており 2011年（平成23年）3月11日(金)東北地方太平洋沖地震（とうほくちほうたいへいようおきじしん）では多くの人が避難をした。

revision.comment: Chiba ryo がページ「[[岩手県久慈市 金刀比羅神社]]」を「[[金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]]」に移動しました: 他の金刀比羅神社と記事名形式を合わせる
revision.text: <big>'''金刀比羅神社（久慈市）'''</big> 金刀比羅神社（ことひらじんじゃ）は、岩手県久慈市湊町にある神社。 こんぴらさんの名称で親しまれている。 高台に鎮座し久慈湾を正面に望み、初日の出を拝むには最高の場所である。また津波避難場所として指定されており 2011年（平成23年）3月11日(金)東北地方太平洋沖地震（とうほくちほうたいへいようおきじしん）では多くの人が避難をした。

revision.text:
金刀比羅神社_(久慈市)
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

金刀比羅神社_(久慈市)
[[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

[[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Kuji)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Konpira Shrine (Kuji)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Konpira Shrine (Kuji)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Kuji)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

(Kuji)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|Thumbnail|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

(Kuji)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)]] {{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|thumb|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = qq.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

[[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|thumb|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = qq.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命}} {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March qq, 20qq, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

{{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

{{Cite sources clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuji-konpira.jpg|thumb|Sanctuary]] | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 {{citation needed|date=October 2015}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=draft}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Great East Japan Earthquake|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.
== References == <references /> {{Pref-substub|pref=Iwate Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|qq=|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|qq=|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

== References ==
<references />

'''Kotohira Shrine''' ({{nihongo|金刀比羅神社|ことひらじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji City]], Iwate Prefecture, Japan. It is affectionately known as {{nihongo|"Konpira-san"|こんぴらさん}}.

The shrine is situated on a hill, offering a scenic view of Kuji Bay. It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|qq=|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|[[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]]|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|qq=|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|qq=|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.
