'''Kotohira Shrine''' ({{nihongo|金刀比羅神社|ことひらじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji City]], Iwate Prefecture, Japan. It is affectionately known as {{nihongo|"Konpira-san"|こんぴらさん}}.

The shrine is situated on a hill, offering a scenic view of Kuji Bay. It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|[[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]]|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|[[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]]|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. '''Kotohira Shrine''' ({{nihongo|金刀比羅神社|ことひらじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji City]], Iwate Prefecture, Japan. It is affectionately known as {{nihongo|"Konpira-san"|こんぴらさん}}.

The shrine is situated on a hill, offering a scenic view of Kuji Bay. It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|[[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]]|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' ({{nihongo|金刀比羅神社|ことひらじんじゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji City]], Iwate Prefecture, Japan. It is affectionately known as {{nihongo|"Konpira-san"|こんぴらさん}}.

The shrine is situated on a hill, offering a scenic view of Kuji Bay. It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|[[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]]|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|[[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]]|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."

The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|Great East Japan Earthquake]] on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

{{Unreferenced clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuji-konpira.jpg | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji, Iwate|en|Kuji, Iwate|WD=Q695911|lt=Kuji}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kuji|WD=Q695911}} | coordinates = {{Coord|40.203194|N|141.791306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji, Iwate|en|Kuji, Iwate|WD=Q695911|lt=Kuji}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

{{Unreferenced clearly|date=April 3, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuji-konpira.jpg | location = Minatomachi 13-90, {{ill|Kuji, Iwate|en|Kuji, Iwate|WD=Q695911|lt=Kuji}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}} | Latitude degree = 40 | Latitude minute = 12 | Latitude second = 11.5 | Longitude degree = 141 | Longitude minute = 47 | Longitude second = 28.7 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Sacred object = | Classification = | Established = 1718 | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 10 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kuji|WD=Q695911}} | coordinates = {{Coord|40.203194|N|141.791306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji, Iwate|en|Kuji, Iwate|WD=Q695911|lt=Kuji}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.

{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira '''Kotohira Shrine''' ({{nihongo|金刀比羅神社|ことひらじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kuji, Iwate|en|Kuji, Iwate|WD=Q695911|lt=Kuji City}}, Iwate Prefecture, Japan. It is affectionately known as {{nihongo|"Konpira-san"|こんぴらさん}}.
The shrine is situated on a hill, offering a scenic view of Kuji Bay. It is designated as a {{ill|tsunami evacuation site|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsunami evacuation site|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=津波ハザードマップ|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Kuji City|accessdate=2015-10-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf|archive-date=2016-03-04}}</ref> and during the {{nihongo|{{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake}}|東日本大震災}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge there.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Kuji, Iwate|en|Kuji, Iwate|WD=Q695911|lt=Kuji|1=Kuji, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, in Minatomachi. It is affectionately known as "Konpira-san."
The shrine is situated on elevated ground, overlooking {{ill|Kuji Bay|ja|久慈湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Bay|1=Kuji Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369340}}. It also serves as a designated {{ill|Tsunami evacuation facility|ja|津波避難施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami evacuation facility|1=tsunami evacuation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17177367}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |format=PDF |title=Tsunami Hazard Map |publisher=Kuji City |accessdate=October 28, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304213804/http://bousai.city.kuji.iwate.jp/contents/pc/pdf/hazardmap/jishintsunami/07.pdf |archive-date=March 4, 2016}}</ref>. During the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} on March 11, 2011, many people sought refuge at the shrine.
